title: Pakruder Worm's New Sound
summary: Hattie the Mouse discovers a tiny worm named Pakruder outside her workshop who is struggling to find her own unique sound. Because the worm can only squeak like a mouse, Hattie takes her on a journey to a large house filled with mice friends to brainstorm ideas. After testing out several whimsical sounds like 'squamp-zam-zim' and 'bamp-pa-deo,' the group finally discovers the perfect sound: 'bam-a-lamp-sam!' The friends celebrate by building a massive Lego ice cream tower and sharing a nap before heading home for the night.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Okay, can you tell me a story about Hattie?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A story always about Hattie. Hmm. What should we do for tonight's story about Hattie? One bright sunny morning, Hattie the Mouse was in her workshop and she opened the door to go outside.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And just when she opened the door, she heard a funny sound. It was a little squeak-squeak-squeak. And she looked straight ahead out the door and she didn't see anything or anybody. And she looked to the left and there was nothing there. She looked to the right, she didn't see anything. She looked up... Huh. Where else could Hattie look?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hattie, there's no mice around.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: There's no mice around. But...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Just... us.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It must be somebody else squeaking, right down at her feet. And she looked down at her feet, and who did she see? Someone really small.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um... I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Was it a slug or a bug...?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, keep telling the story and then you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, well I need your help. Was it a worm or a snake...?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, Dad, keep telling the story and then you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. Hattie looked down at her feet and she saw a worm. And the worm was going squeak-squeak-squeak. And Hattie said, 'Excuse me worm, what a beautiful day, but why are you saying squeak-squeak?' And what did the worm say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I need a sound for myself.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The worm said, 'I need a sound for myself, I don't know what sound to make.' And Hattie said, 'Well, that's a very big problem, but I think I can help you solve it.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Hattie said, 'Well worm, nice to meet you. What's your name?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: My name is Pakruder Worm.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, nice to meet you, Pakruder Worm, said Hattie. I think I can help you find your sound. So Hattie picked up Pakruder Worm and put her on her shoulder. And who did she visit first?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She met Lily, Lisca, Jetmans, Timms, Victor, Chester and Wilson.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, that's a lot of mice. Lily Mouse and all of her friends were playing in the living room with Legos. And they said, 'Hello Hattie!' And Hattie said, 'Hi, I have a new friend here. It's this little worm. But we have a problem.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And the worm said, 'Yeah, I need to find a sound.' And what did Lily the Mouse say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um... I have the sound that you can make. It's a squamp-zam-zim sound.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A squamp-zam-zim sound! Let me try it out, said the worm. Hmm, I like that sound very much, but I don't think it's my sound. Does anybody else have any other sounds?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Bamp-bamp-boo.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Bamp-bam-bum.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Bamp-pa-deo.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dump-pa-deer.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Bip-bam-bims.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Bip-bam-bims! Hey, I think that's it! Hooray!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Bam-a-lamp-sam.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Bam-a-lamp-sam! That's the worm sound. That's my worm sound, Bam-a-lamp-sam!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That's good.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: We're glad that we found your sound, Wormy. What are you building out of your Legos?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A tall-tall ice cream.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A tall-tall ice cream? And Hattie came over to play Legos, and she picked an orange Lego and she put it on top of the ice cream. And it became even taller.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Zzz... and Zzz... and when they're done lunch they went Zzz and Zzz.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Zzz... and take them over... Zzz... and one more time... Zzz... at time minute was down now I not feel sick anymore than the rabbit.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, the rabbit's not feeling sick anymore, that's good. And then Hattie the Mouse and her new worm friend, who had found her sound, were very happy. And they both went home to their own houses to have dinner and go to sleep. And that's the end of our story.

characters.name: Hattie
characters.description: A clever mouse milliner and engineer of whimsy, she is a resourceful spirit who crafts exquisite hats and soulful portraits within her hollow tree stump workshop. Though a formidable presence famously counted among the thirty legendary cats, she is currently confined to her bed by illness, her usual treks through saturated woodlands for rare tape and lollipops temporarily halted. Even in her malaise, her sharp mind lingers on a recent, stinging disagreement with Dimitar over a fish, a rare shadow cast upon a heart otherwise known for its boundless generosity and creative light.

characters.name: Pakruder Worm
characters.description: A tiny worm who was initially squeaking like a mouse but searched for her own unique sound.

characters.name: Lily
characters.description: The world’s tallest mouse is a guardian scholar of monumental power and studious grace, serving as the living pillar of a communal house where he hoists the massive roof with peerless strength. Between shifts at the Pepperoni Street tape store and classes at the local school, this friend of Mistress Mouse finds quiet joy building with Legos or coloring in the baby lounge alongside his many brothers. Adorned in his signature shell necklace and a fresh bespoke hat from Haddy, he remains a gentle soul whose immense physical duty is matched only by his dedicated, playful heart.

characters.name: Lisca
characters.description: One of the many mice friends living in the big house.

characters.name: Jetmans
characters.description: A mouse resident of the large mouse house.

characters.name: Timms
characters.description: A mouse who lives and plays with Legos in the big house.

characters.name: Victor
characters.description: Clad in blue shorts and a purple shirt, Lily’s brother is a tiny baby mouse and spirited cosmic stowaway who once served as a royal gardener within the Magic Forest’s glitter-shimmering houses. Now a rugged driver of a tow truck in the big car race, he balances his mechanical duties with a quest for a celestial watering can and the composition of percussive ‘bamp-bam-bum’ beats for space-faring worms. Though he treasures the sparkly art of Uncle Grisco and Haddy, his high-speed adventures often result in muddy messes that necessitate restorative baths, leaving him in singular pursuit of the perfect hat to complete his interstellar ensemble.

characters.name: Chester
characters.description: A mouse of many suggestions who auditions a repertoire of sounds for his worm friend, now counted among the many figures awaiting a hat of his own.

characters.name: Wilson
characters.description: One of the group of mice living in the shared home.

characters.name: Sherry
characters.description: A mouse mentioned as part of the group playing in the living room.

characters.name: Bepin
characters.description: A mouse who helps the worm find the perfect sound.

characters.name: The Rabbit
characters.description: A secondary character who was feeling sick but recovered by the end of the day.

places.name: Hattie's Workshop
places.description: Tucked within the cozy hollow of a weathered tree, Hattie Mouse’s dwelling serves as a busy, imaginative atelier and social hub where the air hums with the rhythmic snip of scissors. Here, amidst a cheerful clutter of measuring tapes, wet pigments, and scavenged shells, she crafts her signature starry hats from an eclectic mix of fur and feathers. The workshop is bathed in the earthy radiance of her fungal store—a brilliant collection of red, orange, and yellow mushrooms—illuminating the haunting indigo witch painting where Hattie first encountered the squeaking worm. It is in this storied corner, beneath the witch's gaze, that she recently unearthed a mysterious message hidden alongside a cache of sparkly green clover stickers.

places.name: The Forest
places.description: Cloaking the surface of the green planet in a vast mantle of dense greenery, this whispering woodland serves as the lush setting of the 'Forest Friends' chronicle. It is a realm where creatures play eternal hide-and-seek between Hattie’s cozy clinic and the ancestral cradle of Finn’s companions. Along magical trails where Hattie and the Pakruder Worm harvest hat-making ingredients, winding paths veil secret gopher tunnels and hidden mole cottages, forming the essential route toward the wizard's castle. While its sun-drenched threshold remains a sanctuary for the Rainbow Giraffe and Mama Bird, the deeper, darker thickets harbor a somber mystery; it was within these sprawling, uncharted depths—the same eerie expanse where the sick creature was first spotted and Mossy was discovered—that Mole recently vanished into the shadows.

places.name: The Big Mouse House
places.description: A large home where many mice live together and build Lego structures in the living room.
